deductions.reason: The tutor never explains why it is guiding the student through reasoning rather than providing answers directly. There is no moment across any of the three conversations where the tutor articulates that this approach supports learning, retention, or skill development.

deductions.evidence_turns: Transcript 1 Turn 8-9, Transcript 2 Turn 6-7, Transcript 3 Turn 7-8
deductions.reason: In all three transcripts, when students make meaningful improvements (e.g., developing a defensible rating rubric, correctly identifying multi-sector stressors, or clarifying their reasoning on heat), the tutor moves directly to the next task without prompting any reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the improved approach worked.

grade.overview: The tutor performs strongly across all three transcripts. Socratic method is consistently applied: the tutor never gives final answers, always asks the student to reason first, and builds on student responses. Scaffolding is well-executed with progressive questioning. The main weaknesses are the absence of any meta-learning rationale (the tutor never explains why it guides rather than tells) and the consistent failure to prompt reflection after student breakthroughs. No other significant deductions were warranted.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 is clean across all three transcripts: the tutor never provides submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently asks students to derive conclusions before confirming them. 1.2 is also clean: the tutor builds on student progress at each turn and diagnoses confusion before responding. For 1.3, two deductions apply across all transcripts: (1) the tutor never explains its pedagogical rationale to the student, violating 1.3.A.a; and (2) whenever students improve (e.g., Transcript 1 Turn 8-9, Transcript 2 Turn 6-7, Transcript 3 Turn 7-8), the tutor moves on without prompting reflection on what changed, violating 1.3.C.a. Process feedback (1.3.B) is generally present and specific enough to avoid deduction. Dialogue quality and communication quality are both strong with no evidence of repetition, drift, or tone issues.
